% Report Title
{\Huge\textcolor{corporateblue}{\textbf{Control Risks Assessment Report}}}\\[1cm]

% Model Information Box

\draw[corporateblue, thick, rounded corners] (0,0) rectangle (12,4);
\fill[corporateblue!10, rounded corners] (0,0) rectangle (12,4);
\node[text width=11cm, align=center] at (6,2) {
    {\Large\textbf{Model Information}}\\[0.5cm]
    {\large Model Name: \textbf{\modelname}}\\[0.2cm]
    {\large Model Type: \textbf{\modeltype}}\\[0.2cm]
    {\large Business Unit: \textbf{\businessunit}}
};
\end{tikzpicture}\\[1cm]

% Report Metadata Box
\begin{tikzpicture}
\draw[corporategray, thick, rounded corners] (0,0) rectangle (12,3);
\fill[corporategray!10, rounded corners] (0,0) rectangle (12,3);
\node[text width=11cm, align=center] at (6,1.5) {
    {\large Report ID: \textbf{\reportid}}\\[0.2cm]
    {\large Generation Date: \textbf{\reportdate}}\\[0.2cm]
    {\large Overall Status: \textbf{\textcolor{criticalred}{\overallstatus}}}\\[0.2cm]
    {\large Risk Score: \textbf{\riskscore}}
};
\end{tikzpicture}\\[1cm]

% Executive Summary Box
\begin{tikzpicture}
\draw[criticalred, thick, rounded corners] (0,0) rectangle (12,2.5);
\fill[criticalred!10, rounded corners] (0,0) rectangle (12,2.5);
\node[text width=11cm, align=center] at (6,1.25) {
    {\Large\textbf{Executive Summary}}\\[0.3cm]
    {\large Total Risks: \textbf{\totalrisks} | Total Controls: \textbf{\totalcontrols}}\\[0.2cm]
    {\large Critical Risks: \textbf{\criticalrisks} | High Risks: \textbf{\highrisks}}
};
\end{tikzpicture}

\vfill

% Confidentiality Notice
\begin{center}
\textcolor{corporategray}{\small\textit{CONFIDENTIAL - This report contains sensitive risk assessment information}}\\
\textcolor{corporategray}{\small\textit{Distribution limited to authorized personnel only}}
\end{center}

\end{titlepage}
}

\hline
#2 & #3 & #4 & #5 & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
}

\newcommand{\statusbox}[2]{
\begin{tikzpicture}[baseline=(current bounding box.center)]
\draw[#1, thick, rounded corners] (0,0) rectangle (3,0.8);
\fill[#1!20, rounded corners] (0,0) rectangle (3,0.8);
\node at (1.5,0.4) {\textbf{#2}};
\end{tikzpicture}
}

%% Executive Summary
\section{Executive Summary}

\subsection{Risk Assessment Overview}

This Control Risks Assessment Report provides a comprehensive analysis of the model risk control framework for \textbf{\modelname}. The assessment covers \textbf{\totalrisks} control risks distributed across \textbf{7 model lifecycle stages}, with a total of \textbf{\totalcontrols} individual controls.

\begin{table}[H]
\centering
\caption{Executive Summary - Key Metrics}
\begin{tabular}{|l|r|l|}
\hline
\textbf{Metric} & \textbf{Value} & \textbf{Status} \\
\hline
Total Risks & \totalrisks & \statusbox{corporateblue}{Complete} \\
\hline
Total Controls & \totalcontrols & \statusbox{corporateblue}{Assessed} \\
\hline
Average Controls per Risk & \avgcontrols & \statusbox{corporategray}{Analyzed} \\
\hline
Overall Risk Status & \overallstatus & \statusbox{criticalred}{\overallstatus} \\
\hline
Risk Score & \riskscore & \statusbox{criticalred}{High Risk} \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection{Risk Distribution Summary}

The risk portfolio demonstrates a concentration in critical and high-severity risks, requiring immediate management attention:

\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2cm]
\item \textcolor{criticalred}{\textbf{Critical Risks:}} \criticalrisks\ risks (\textbf{75.0\%}) - Immediate action required
\item \textcolor{highorange}{\textbf{High Risks:}} \highrisks\ risks (\textbf{25.0\%}) - Priority mitigation needed
\item \textcolor{mediumyellow}{\textbf{Medium Risks:}} \mediumrisks\ risks - Standard monitoring
\item \textcolor{lowlightgreen}{\textbf{Low Risks:}} \lowrisks\ risks - Routine oversight
\item \textcolor{minimalgreen}{\textbf{Minimal Risks:}} \minimalrisks\ risks - Basic controls
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Priority Recommendations}

Based on the risk assessment, the following priority actions are recommended:

\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2cm]
\item \textbf{Immediate Attention:} Address \criticalrisks\ critical risks with emergency mitigation strategies
\item \textbf{Priority Mitigation:} Develop comprehensive mitigation plans for \highrisks\ high risks
\item \textbf{Governance Strengthening:} Enhance oversight for \governancerisks\ governance risks
\item \textbf{Monitoring Enhancement:} Strengthen monitoring procedures for operational risks
\item \textbf{Compliance Alignment:} Ensure full regulatory compliance across all risk categories
\end{enumerate}

\section{Stage-Based Risk Analysis}

\subsection{Planning Stage Analysis}

The Planning stage encompasses \planningrisks\ risks with \planningcontrols\ controls, representing the foundation of model risk management.

\subsubsection{Planning Stage Risk Profile}
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Risk Count:} \planningrisks\ risks (94.0\% of total risks)
\item \textbf{Total Controls:} \planningcontrols\ controls (14.5\% of total controls)
\item \textbf{Average Controls per Risk:} 3.1 controls
\item \textbf{Critical Risks:} 60 risks requiring immediate attention
\item \textbf{High Risks:} 19 risks requiring priority mitigation
\end{itemize}

\subsubsection{Planning Stage Recommendations}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Strengthen business requirements definition processes
\item Enhance stakeholder engagement procedures
\item Improve risk assessment methodologies during planning
\item Establish comprehensive documentation standards
\item Implement robust approval processes
\end{enumerate}

\subsection{Development Stage Analysis}

\section{Recommendations and Action Plan}

\subsection{Priority Recommendations}

Based on the comprehensive risk assessment, the following recommendations are prioritized:

\subsubsection{Immediate Actions (0-30 days)}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Address \criticalrisks\ critical risks with emergency mitigation strategies
\item Establish executive oversight for high-risk areas
\item Implement immediate monitoring enhancements
\item Initiate compliance gap remediation
\item Activate escalation procedures for critical findings
\end{enumerate}

\subsubsection{Short-term Actions (30-90 days)}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Develop comprehensive mitigation plans for \highrisks\ high risks
\item Strengthen governance framework implementation
\item Enhance validation procedures and documentation
\item Implement improved monitoring and alerting systems
\item Establish regular risk review cycles
\end{enumerate}

\subsubsection{Medium-term Actions (3-6 months)}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Complete governance framework enhancements
\item Implement comprehensive training programs
\item Establish advanced monitoring capabilities
\item Complete compliance framework alignment
\item Implement risk management automation
\end{enumerate}

\subsubsection{Long-term Actions (6+ months)}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Implement advanced risk analytics capabilities
\item Establish predictive risk monitoring
\item Complete risk management maturity enhancement
\item Implement continuous improvement processes
\item Establish risk management center of excellence
\end{enumerate}

\item External consulting: \$100K - \$200K

%% Appendices
\appendix

\section{Risk Methodology}

\subsection{Risk Categorization}
Risks are classified into ten primary categories based on their nature and impact:
\begin{itemize}
\item Development Risks: Risks during model development
\item Validation Risks: Risks in model validation processes
\item Implementation Risks: Risks during model deployment
\item Monitoring Risks: Risks in ongoing model monitoring
\item Governance Risks: Risks in governance and oversight
\item Data Risks: Risks related to data quality and management
\item Model Risks: Risks inherent in model design and performance
\item Business Risks: Risks affecting business operations
\item Regulatory Risks: Risks related to regulatory compliance
\item Technical Risks: Risks in technical infrastructure
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Severity Assessment}
Risk severity is determined based on:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Critical:} Severe impact, immediate action required
\item \textbf{High:} Significant impact, priority attention needed
\item \textbf{Medium:} Moderate impact, standard monitoring
\item \textbf{Low:} Minor impact, routine oversight
\item \textbf{Minimal:} Negligible impact, basic controls
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Control Assessment}
Controls are evaluated based on:
\begin{itemize}
\item Effectiveness in risk mitigation
\item Implementation completeness
\item Monitoring and maintenance
\item Documentation quality
\item Compliance alignment
\end{itemize}

\section{Data Sources and Assumptions}

\subsection{Data Sources}
\begin{itemize}
\item Control risks YAML configuration file
\item Model documentation and specifications
\item Regulatory guidance and requirements
\item Industry best practices and standards
\item Internal policies and procedures
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Key Assumptions}
\begin{itemize}
\item Risk data is current and accurate
\item Control implementations are as documented
\item Regulatory requirements are properly interpreted
\item Risk assessments reflect current environment
\item Mitigation strategies are feasible
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Limitations}
\begin{itemize}
\item Analysis based on available documentation
\item Risk assessments are point-in-time
\item External factors may impact risk profile
\item Implementation effectiveness may vary
\item Regulatory changes may affect requirements
\end{itemize}

\section{Glossary of Terms}

\begin{description}
\item[Control Risk] A specific risk that may impact model performance or compliance
\item[Model Stage] A phase in the model development and management lifecycle
\item[Risk Category] A classification of risks based on their nature and source
\item[Risk Severity] The level of impact and urgency associated with a risk
\item[Control] A measure or procedure designed to mitigate or manage a risk
\item[Mitigation Strategy] An approach to reduce or eliminate risk impact
\item[Governance] The framework for oversight, decision-making, and accountability
\item[Compliance] Adherence to regulatory requirements and internal policies
\item[Validation] The process of verifying model accuracy and appropriateness
\item[Monitoring] Ongoing surveillance of model performance and risk indicators
\end{description}

\section{Supporting Documentation}

\subsection{Risk Register Export}
Complete risk data is available in the accompanying JSON export file:
\texttt{control\_risks\_report\_data.json}

\subsection{Control Mapping}
Detailed control mappings are maintained in the YAML configuration:
\texttt{config/control\_risks.yaml}

\subsection{Compliance Matrix}
Regulatory compliance mappings are documented in:
\texttt{compliance\_requirements\_matrix.xlsx}
